PHY.name: Physics & Chemistry
PHY.description: Classical and modern physics, quantum mechanics, thermodynamics, chemistry fundamentals, materials science

BIO.name: Biology & Life Sciences
BIO.description: Genetics, molecular biology, ecology, evolution, cell biology — distinct from clinical Healthcare

ENV.name: Environmental Science
ENV.description: Climate science, ecosystems, pollution, biodiversity, environmental policy

CSC.name: Computer Science & AI
CSC.description: Algorithms, systems architecture, machine learning concepts, cybersecurity, software engineering

SPC.name: Space & Astronomy
SPC.description: Cosmology, planetary science, space technology, observation methods, space policy

ENG.name: Engineering
ENG.description: Structural, mechanical, electrical, civil — design principles, safety standards, materials

MAT.name: Mathematics & Statistics
MAT.description: Formal mathematics, probability, statistical methods, applied mathematics, numerical analysis

rules.name: Require Source URL
rules.description: Every fragment must have a source_url from a credible scientific source

rules.name: Require Year
rules.description: Every fragment must have a year indicating when the information was verified

rules.name: Peer-Reviewed Preference
rules.description: Scientific fragments should prioritize peer-reviewed sources (ArXiv, Nature, Science, IEEE, etc.)

rules.name: No Stale Scientific Information
rules.description: Fragments older than 4 years cannot exceed credibility score of 60

rules.name: Three Roles Required
rules.description: Every topic must have definition, counter_argument, and latest_data roles

rules.name: Empirical Claims Only
rules.description: Science fragments must describe empirical findings, not philosophical interpretations
